title: Which City Receives the Monsoon First in India? - Adda247
article_text:
Every year in summer temperature soar across the India and millions of people are eagerly waiting for the arrival of the monsoons. As framers look in to skies for rain which will nourish their crops and city people hope for the from the scorching summer heat.

Most of news channels, weather experts and travelers closely track one important question: Where does the monsoon arrive first in India?

The answer is more interesting than many people realize it. In India one region receives the first monsoon showers and another holds the distinction of being the first city on the Indian mainland to welcome the rainy season.

Understanding this journey helps to explain why the monsoon is often called the lifeline of India.

Which City Receives the Monsoon First in India?

The first city on the Indian mainland to receive the southwest monsoon is the Thiruvananthapuram, the capital of Kerala.

However if India’s island territories are included then Andaman and Nicobar Islands receive the monsoon even earlier usually around the May 22 just several days before monsoon reaches to Kerala.

So the distinction is the,

First in India overall: Andaman and Nicobar Islands

First city on the Indian mainland: Thiruvananthapuram (Capital of Keralam)

Traditionally, the monsoon reaches to the Kerala around June 1st and marking the official beginning of the southwest monsoon season in India.

Why Does Kerala Receive the Monsoon First?

The geographical location of Kerala gives unique advantage. As it is situated at the southwestern tip of the Indian peninsula and Kerala become the first major landmass encountered by moisture-laden winds traveling form the Arabian Sea.

Another important factor was the presence of the Western Ghats. These western ghats mountains act as a natural barrier and forcing the moist air to rise and cool and resulting in the significant rainfall across Kerala.

This combination of the location and topography makes Kerala as the natural gateway for India’s monsoon.

Join Our WhatsApp Channel For Free PDF

How Does the Monsoon Travel Across India?

Once the southwest monsoon reaches to Kerala, it begins a gradual journey across the country.

The rain bearing winds moves northward and eastward and covering the different regions in various phases. The coastal areas receive rainfall first followed by the central and northern India.

The monsoon typically follows this type of progression,

Region/City	Normal Arrival Period
Andaman and Nicobar Islands	Around May 22
Thiruvananthapuram (Kerala)	Around June 1
Goa and Coastal Karnataka	June 5–10
Mumbai	Around June 11
Kolkata	June 10–15
Delhi	June 27–July 1
Entire Rajasthan	Around July 15

By the mid-July, the southwest monsoon usually covers the entire part of the country.

What Did the IMD Say About the 2026 Monsoon?

According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD) the southwest monsoon has reached the Andaman and Nicobar Islands on May 18, 2026 which is earlier than usual.

The weather agency also indicated that Kerala could receive the monsoon before its normal date of June 1st and marking an early monsoon year.

The IMD forecast for seasonal rainfall stand at around 92% of the Long Period Average (LPA) and suggesting slightly below-normal rainfall but not the drought conditions.

How Does the IMD Declare the Onset of Monsoon?

The official onset of the monsoon over Kerala is not just declared simply because it rains at that part.

The IMD follows the specific scientific criteria, including the,

At least 60% of designated weather stations reporting rainfall.
Rainfall of a 2.5 mm or more at these stations for two consecutive days.
Suitable wind patterns and the atmospheric conditions.

Only after these conditions are met does the IMD officially announce that the onset of the southwest monsoon.

Interesting Facts About India’s Monsoon

Nearly 70% of the India’s annual rainfall comes during the southwest monsoon season.

Agriculture in large parts of India heavily relies on timely monsoon rains for the crop production.

A good monsoon often boosts the agricultural output, rural income and overall economic growth of country.

While June 1 is the normal onset date for the Kerala but the monsoon may arrive earlier or later depending on to the weather conditions.

From Kerala to northwestern India the Indian monsoon usually takes about six weeks to cover the entire country.

title: Southwest Monsoon Arrives In Kerala: IMD - The Global Kashmir
article_text:
NEW DELHI : The southwest monsoon set over Kerala on Thursday, according to the India Meteorological Department (IMD).

Typically, the monsoon arrives in Kerala around June 1, which marks the beginning of the Southwest monsoon season (June-September).

“The southwest monsoon has further advanced into the remaining parts of southwest and southeast Arabian Sea, some parts of westcentral and eastcentral Arabian Sea, entire Lakshadweep islands, Kerala and Mahe, some parts of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, remaining parts of Comorin area, southeast Bay of Bengal and some more parts of southwest, westcentral, eastcentral and northeast Bay of Bengal today the 4th June, 2026,” the IMD said.

Previously, the weather department had predicted that the monsoon would begin over Kerala on May 26; however, the arrival was delayed. Last week, the department revised its forecast for seasonal rainfall, indicating that it would be below normal.

The IMD expects rainfall in India to be around 90% of the long-period average (LPA) this year.

The LPA refers to the rainfall recorded in a specific region over a defined period, such as a month or season, averaged over a long duration, typically 30 to 50 years.

The long-period average (LPA) of seasonal rainfall over the country as a whole, based on data from 1971 to 2020, is 87 cm. If the monsoon season sees less than 90 pc of LPA rainfall, the IMD classifies it as ‘deficient’.

One reason behind the below-normal rainfall could be the emergence of El Nino conditions, which lead to less rain during the monsoon in the country.

Currently, neutral El Nino-Southern Oscillation conditions are transitioning towards El Nino conditions over the equatorial Pacific region.

The IMD said that El Nino conditions are likely to be weak in June, and moderate to strong in September. (Agencies)

title: NC to protest on Monsoon session’s opening day over statehood demand in New Delhi - The Kashmir Horizon
article_text:
After Dachigam strategy meet, JKNC announces Delhi protest for Statehood

CM Omar leads legislators and allies at off-site retreat

Srinagar: The ruling National Conference (NC) on Wednesday announced that its legislators, MPs and alliance partners will stage a protest in New Delhi on the opening day of Parliament’s Monsoon Session to press for the restoration of Jammu and Kashmir’s statehood and constitutional guarantees.

The decision was taken at a day-long meeting of NC legislators and supporting independents chaired by Chief Minister Omar Abdullah at an off-site location in the Dachigam area on the outskirts of Srinagar.

The gathering assumed political significance as it came amid assertions by Leader of Opposition Sunil Sharma that the NC-led government was facing internal dissent and could collapse at any time.

Addressing reporters after the meeting, NC chief spokesperson Tanvir Sadiq said the party’s entire legislative wing, including MPs, MLAs and allies, would participate in the protest.

“The National Conference has decided that on the first day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, our Legislative Party, including MPs, MLAs and allies, will go to Delhi to protest. Our primary demand will be the restoration of statehood and constitutional guarantees for Jammu and Kashmir,” Sadiq said.

He said the party leadership believed the time had come to take the issue directly to the national capital.

“It is time for our leadership to go to Delhi, talk to the people there and take back what has been taken away from us,” he added.

The announcement comes at a time when the NC government has been facing increasing political pressure over the restoration of statehood, one of its principal electoral commitments. The issue has also regained momentum following the Centre’s engagement with groups in Ladakh seeking constitutional safeguards and protections for the Union Territory.

Earlier in the day, legislators invited to a meeting at the Chief Minister’s official residence found themselves boarding mini-buses that transported them to the Dachigam area, away from the media spotlight.

The unusual venue arrangement immediately triggered political speculation, particularly because it coincided with opposition claims that several NC legislators were unhappy with the government.

Sharing photographs of legislators travelling together in buses, CM Omar described the gathering as a review exercise aimed at assessing the government’s performance since assuming office.

“We are off for an off-site to spend the day taking stock of the last 19 months — the good, the not so good and everything in between,” Abdullah posted on social media platform X.

As speculation mounted over reports that the venue had been shifted at the last moment, the Chief Minister later clarified that the off-site meeting had been planned in advance.

“I’m sorry to disappoint you but it has NOT been shifted at the last moment. It was always my intention to conduct this meeting off-site and all arrangements had been made days in advance at the location originally chosen by me,” he said.

According to party leaders, the meeting brought together NC legislators, Members of Parliament from both Houses, CPI(M) leader Mohammad Yusuf Tarigami and four independent legislators supporting the government.

The meeting reviewed the government’s functioning over the past 19 months and discussed a range of issues, including development, public welfare, unemployment, drug abuse, the economy and governance challenges across Jammu, Kashmir, PirPanjal and Chenab Valley regions.

However, participants indicated that the restoration of statehood and constitutional safeguards dominated the discussions.

JKNC Spokesman Sadiq described the atmosphere at the meeting as positive and said legislators engaged in detailed deliberations on issues confronting the region.

“There was a review of the progress made by this government. But the most important discussion was on statehood and constitutional guarantees,” he said.

The gathering was also viewed as an attempt by the party leadership to consolidate its ranks amid sustained attacks from the opposition and growing public expectations regarding the fulfilment of promises related to Jammu and Kashmir’s political status.

Notably absent from the meeting was Srinagar Lok Sabha MP Aga Syed Ruhullah Mehdi, who has publicly voiced differences with the party leadership over its approach to the restoration of special status and constitutional rights.

When questioned about opposition claims regarding internal dissension, Sadiq avoided a direct confrontation but asserted that the party remained united and focused on issues concerning the people of Jammu and Kashmir.

The meeting also discussed governance priorities and future political strategy, although party leaders maintained that statehood would remain the central issue in the coming months.

title: Southwest Monsoon Arrives in Kerala, IMD Announces Onset of Rainy Season - DD News
article_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Thursday officially declared the onset of the Southwest Monsoon over Kerala, marking the beginning of the country’s four-month rainy season.

The monsoon arrived on June 4, three days later than its normal onset date of June 1. According to the weather department, the monsoon has covered the entire state of Kerala, Lakshadweep and Mahe, while also advancing over the remaining parts of the southwest and southeast Arabian Sea.

The IMD said conditions remain favourable for the further advance of the monsoon over the next two to three days. It is expected to progress into more parts of the central Arabian Sea, Goa, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, as well as additional areas of the Bay of Bengal and the northeastern states.

The weather department attributed the onset to increased convective cloud activity over the southeast Arabian Sea and strengthening westerly winds in the lower atmospheric levels. Widespread rainfall, including isolated heavy showers, has been recorded across Kerala during the past two days.

The IMD has forecast isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall, ranging between 7 cm and 20 cm, over Kerala during the next six to seven days. Isolated heavy rainfall is also likely over parts of Tamil Nadu and Karnataka during the same period.

The arrival of the monsoon is closely watched as it plays a crucial role in India’s agriculture sector, water resources and overall economy. The seasonal rains provide nearly 70 per cent of the country’s annual rainfall and support the cultivation of key kharif crops.

Meanwhile, the IMD has projected that India is likely to receive rainfall amounting to around 90 per cent of the Long Period Average (LPA) during the monsoon season this year.

In the national capital, Delhi recorded a minimum temperature of 29.7 degrees Celsius on Thursday. The weather office has issued a yellow alert for rain and thunderstorms in the city.

-ANI

title: Weather Update: आज देशात मान्सूनची दमदार एंट्री! 17 राज्यांमध्ये मुसळधार पाऊस आणि वादळाचा इशारा - Marathi Jagran
article_text:
डिजिटल डेस्क, नवी दिल्ली. Weather Update: देशात मान्सूनचा हंगाम सुरू होणार आहे. हवामान विभागाच्या अंदाजानुसार, केरळ आणि तामिळनाडूमध्ये आज कोणत्याही क्षणी मान्सून दाखल होऊ शकतो. येत्या 24 तासांत पंजाब, हरियाणा, दिल्ली, राजस्थान, मध्य प्रदेश, पश्चिम उत्तर प्रदेश, महाराष्ट्र आणि दक्षिण भारतासह अनेक भागांमध्ये मुसळधार पावसाची शक्यता आहे.
राजस्थानच्या काही भागांमध्ये धुळीची वादळे आणि गारपिटीची शक्यता आहे. केरळ आणि कर्नाटकमध्ये मुसळधार पावसाचा अंदाज आहे. अंदमान आणि निकोबार बेटांवरही मुसळधार पाऊस पडेल. तापमानात आणखी घट होण्याची शक्यता असून, त्यामुळे उकाड्यापासून काहीसा दिलासा मिळेल.
भारतीय हवामान विभागाच्या अंदाजानुसार, पुढील 5 ते 7 दिवसांत केरळच्या अनेक भागांमध्ये मुसळधार ते अति मुसळधार पावसाचा अंदाज आहे. काही ठिकाणी 7 ते 20 सेंटिमीटर पाऊस पडू शकतो.
केरळमध्ये अतिवृष्टीमुळे सखल भागात पाणी साचणे, नद्यांना पूर येणे आणि भूस्खलन होण्याची शक्यता असल्याने हवामान विभागाने अनेक जिल्ह्यांसाठी ऑरेंज अलर्ट जारी केला आहे.
दिल्ली एनसीआरमधील हवामानाची स्थितीआज दिल्लीत कमाल तापमान 38 ते 40 अंश सेल्सिअसच्या दरम्यान राहण्याची शक्यता असून, कडक उन्हापासून काहीसा दिलासा मिळेल. आकाश अंशतः ढगाळ राहील आणि संध्याकाळी किंवा रात्री धुळीची वादळे व मेघगर्जनेसह पावसाची शक्यता असून, ताशी 50 किमी वेगाने सोसाट्याचा वारा वाहण्याची शक्यता आहे.
उत्तराखंड आणि हिमाचल प्रदेशमध्ये गारपिटीची वादळे होतील.पश्चिमी वाऱ्यांच्या प्रभावामुळे डोंगराळ राज्यांमध्ये हवामान सुखद राहील. हिमाचल प्रदेशातील काही भागांसाठी गारपिटीचा इशारा देण्यात आला आहे, तर उत्तराखंडच्या अनेक भागांमध्ये सोसाट्याच्या वाऱ्यासह पाऊस पडण्याची शक्यता आहे.
बिहारमध्ये उष्णता असेलसध्या तरी बिहारमध्ये दमट उष्णतेचा त्रास सुरूच राहणार आहे. हवामान विभागाच्या (IMD) अंदाजानुसार, या आठवड्याच्या अखेरपर्यंत बिहारच्या अनेक जिल्ह्यांमध्ये तीव्र उष्णतेची लाट येण्याची शक्यता आहे. हेही वाचा: Maharashtra Weather Update: पुढील 3 दिवस महत्त्वाचे! संपूर्ण राज्यात वादळी वाऱ्यासह मुसळधार पावसाचा अलर्ट

title: IMD Monsoon Update: Southwest Monsoon Advances, Goa And Maharashtra Next In Line - Oneindia
article_text:
Home / India
IMD Monsoon Update: Southwest Monsoon Advances, Goa And Maharashtra Next In Line
By Madhuri Adnal
 Published: Thursday, June 4, 2026, 13:42 [IST]
  
Add as a preferred source on Google

The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Thursday confirmed that the Southwest Monsoon has advanced further over several parts of the country, covering Kerala, Mahe, Lakshadweep, parts of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, while conditions remain favourable for its continued progress over additional regions in the coming days.

According to the IMD, the monsoon has now advanced into the remaining parts of the Southwest and Southeast Arabian Sea, sections of the west-central and east-central Arabian Sea, the entire Lakshadweep region, Kerala and Mahe, parts of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, and additional areas of the Bay of Bengal.

AI Summary

AI-generated summary, reviewed by editors

The India Meteorological Department confirmed the Southwest Monsoon has advanced further, covering Kerala, Lakshadweep, parts of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, with its onset officially declared over Kerala on June 4. Favourable conditions exist for its continued progress into Goa, Maharashtra, and other regions within days.
Switch To Shorts

The weather agency said the Northern Limit of Monsoon (NLM) currently passes through Mangaluru, Udhagamandalam, Kodaikanal and Thoothukudi, indicating the extent of the monsoon's advance as of June 4.

More Areas Likely To Receive Monsoon Rain

The IMD has forecast favourable conditions for the monsoon to advance further into more parts of the central Arabian Sea, the entire state of Goa, parts of Maharashtra and Andhra Pradesh, additional regions of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu, and some northeastern states over the next two to three days.

This progression is expected to bring widespread rainfall to several regions that have been waiting for the arrival of the rainy season.

Also Read
Southwest Monsoon Arrives In India As IMD Declares Onset Over Kerala
Monsoon Reaches Kerala Three Days Late

The official onset of the Southwest Monsoon over Kerala was declared on June 4, three days later than the normal onset date of June 1.

Earlier forecasts had suggested a possible onset around May 26, but changing atmospheric conditions delayed its arrival. Meteorologists had subsequently revised their forecast, indicating that the monsoon would likely reach Kerala during the first week of June.

What Led To The Monsoon Onset?

According to the IMD, all key meteorological parameters required for declaring the onset of the monsoon over Kerala were satisfied.

During the past two days, convective cloud activity increased significantly over the Southeast Arabian Sea. The depth of westerly winds extended up to 4.5 kilometres above mean sea level, while wind speeds in the lower atmosphere reached around 20 to 25 knots.

The state also recorded widespread rainfall, including isolated spells of heavy rain, over the last two days.

These conditions collectively indicated the establishment of a sustained monsoon circulation pattern, prompting the IMD to officially declare the onset of the Southwest Monsoon over Kerala.

Why The Southwest Monsoon Matters

The Southwest Monsoon is India's primary rainy season and contributes nearly 80 per cent of the country's annual rainfall. Its arrival is closely monitored because it plays a crucial role in agriculture, water availability, power generation and the overall economy.

A timely and well-distributed monsoon helps support the cultivation of major kharif crops such as rice, pulses, cotton and sugarcane, while also replenishing reservoirs and groundwater levels across the country.

With the monsoon now officially underway and advancing steadily, weather experts expect rainfall activity to increase across several parts of western, southern and northeastern India in the coming days.

Published On June 4, 2026

title: Kerala set for June 4 monsoon onset with heavy rain alerts - MSN
article_text:
Heatwave relief: Temperatures are falling by 2–4°C after pre-monsoon showers, prompting IMD to lift heatwave alerts statewide.
Monsoon window: Rains are expected between June 8–13, beginning in southern Telangana districts and spreading northward.
Seasonal risks: Below-normal and uneven rainfall linked to El Niño could challenge agriculture and water management.

title: Aaj ka Mausam 4 June 2026: मॉनसून की दस्तक के बीच IMD का बड़ा अलर्ट, जान लें कहां चलेंगीं 70 KMPH तक हवाएं, किन राज्यों में है बारिश-तूफान का खतरा - InKhabar
article_text:
Aaj ka Mausam 4 June 2026:  उत्तर भारत को छोड़ दें तो देशभर में मॉनसून की गतिविधियां जल्द ही तेज हो सकती हैं. भारतीय मौसम विज्ञान विभाग (IMD) ने गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को दिल्ली, उत्तर प्रदेश, बिहार, राजस्थान, पंजाब, हरियाणा और दक्षिण भारत समेत कई राज्यों में बारिश, आंधी, बिजली गिरने और तेज हवाओं का अलर्ट जारी किया है. इस दौरान कई जगह 70 किमी प्रति घंटा तक हवाएं चल सकती हैं, जिससे गर्मी से राहत मिलेगी.  मौसम विभाग का कहना है कि आने वाले दिनों में मॉनसून तेजी से आगे बढ़ेगा और देश के बड़े हिस्से में मौसम का स्वरूप पूरी तरह बदल सकता है. 4 जून को केरल में मॉनसून दस्तक दे सकता है.
By: JP Yadav | Published: June 4, 2026 7:35:53 AM IST
मौसम विभाग के ताजा अलर्ट के अनुसार, गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को पश्चिमी विक्षोभ के एक्टिव होने से दिल्ली-एनसीआर हल्की बारिश में हो सकती है. गुरुवार को राजधानी दिल्ली और एनसीआर के शहरों में आंशिक रूप से बादल छाए रहेंगे. दोपहर से शाम तक के दौरान हल्की बारिश होने का अलर्ट है. कुछ जगहों पर आंधी की संभावना भी जताई गई है.
दिल्ली से सटे उत्तर प्रदेश में गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को कई जिलों में गरज-चमक के साथ बारिश के आसार हैं. IMD के अनुसार, गुरुवार को राजधानी लखनऊ, उन्नाव, फतेहपुर, बांदा, प्रयागराज, कौशांबी, मिर्जापुर, जौनपुर, आजमगढ़, चित्रकूट, सोनभद्र, वाराणसी, गाजीपुर और कानपुर समेत कई जिलों में आंधी आने का अलर्ट है. वहीं, मौसम विभाग ने पश्चिमी उत्तर प्रदेश के कई जिलों में गरज-चमक के साथ बारिश होने की संभावना जताई है.
बिहार में मौसम का मिजाज बदला हुआ है. IMD की ओर से गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को बिहार के किसी भी जिले के लिए बारिश या फिर आंधी का अलर्ट जारी नहीं किया गया है.
झारखंड में गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को बारिश की संभावना है. झारखंड में 4 को गरज-चमक, बिजली और तेज हवाओं के साथ बारिश की संभावना बनी हुई है. कई इलाकों में 40-50 किमी प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से हवाएं चल सकती हैं.
पर्वतीय राज्य उत्तराखंड में भारी बारिश का अलर्ट जारी किया गया है. मौसम विभाग के मुताबिक, पश्चिमी विक्षोभ के एक्टिव होने से उत्तराखंड में गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को भारी बारिश होने का अलर्ट जारी है. 4 जून को आंधी-बारिश के साथ तेज हवाएं भी चल सकती है. इस दौरान ओलावृष्टि और भूस्खलन का भी खतरा है.
IMD के अनुसार गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को हिमाचल प्रदेश में गरज-चमक, बिजली और 50-60 किमी प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाओं के साथ हल्की से मध्यम बारिश होने की संभावना है. कुछ इलाकों में ओलावृष्टि का भी अलर्ट जारी किया गया है.
राजस्थान में गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को आंधी का अलर्ट जारी है. मौसम विभाग के ताजा पूर्वानुमान में पूर्वी और पश्चिमी राजस्थान में गुरुवार को आंधी, बारिश, बिजली गिरने की संभावना जताई है. इस दौरान 20 से अधिक जिलों में 50-60 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे की रफ्तार से तेज हवाएं चल सकती हैं. कई जगहों पर बारिश के साथ ओले भी गिर सकते हैं.
केरल, तमिलनाडु, कर्नाटक, लक्षद्वीप और पुडुचेरी में मॉनसून का प्रभाव सबसे ज्यादा देखने को मिल सकता है. गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को
कई स्थानों पर भारी से बहुत भारी बारिश की चेतावनी जारी की गई है. तमिलनाडु के 16 जिलों में विशेष अलर्ट जारी है.
गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) से पूर्वोत्तर के राज्यों में शामिल अरुणाचल प्रदेश, असम और मेघालय में मॉनसून की गतिविधियां तेज हो रही हैं. इसके बाद 5 से 8 जून के बीच कई इलाकों में भारी बारिश हो सकती है.
मौसम विभाग की ओर से ताजा पूर्वानुमान जताया गया है कि पंजाब और हरियाणा में 4 से 6 जून के बीच बारिश और गरज-चमक देखने को मिल सकती है. पंजाब के अमृतसर, लुधियाना, पटियाला, जालंधर, मोहाली और गुरदासपुर समेत कई जिलों में गुरुवार (04 जून, 2026) को तेज हवाएं चलने की संभावना है. हरियाणा के कई जिलों में आंधी के साथ हल्की बारिश होने का अलर्ट है.

title: ગુજરાતમાં વરસાદ : ભારતમાં ચોમાસું બેસવાની તૈયારી, કયા જિલ્લાઓમાં આજે ગાજવીજ સાથે વરસાદ પડશે? - BBC
article_text:
ભારતમાં ચોમાસું બેસવાની તૈયારી, આજે ગુજરાતના આ જિલ્લાઓમાં પડી શકે છે ચોમાસા પહેલાંનો વરસાદ

ઇમેજ સ્રોત,
GETTY IMAGES

પ્રકાશિત
6 કલાક પહેલા
વાંચવાનો સમય: 3 મિનિટ

ગુજરાતમાં ઘણા જિલ્લામાં પાછલા અમુક દિવસથી છૂટોછવાયો વરસાદ પડી રહ્યો છે ત્યારે 3 જૂનના રોજ ભારતના હવામાન વિભાગે પ્રેસ રિલીઝ જાહેર કરીને આગામી 24 કલાકમાં કેરળમાં દક્ષિણપશ્ચિમ ચોમાસું બેસે તેવી શક્યતા વ્યક્ત કરી છે.

આ સાથે જ ભારતમાં ટૂંક સમયમાં વિધિવત્ ચોમાસું બેસવાની શક્યતાઓ સેવાઈ રહી છે.

ગુજરાતની વાત કરીએ તો પાછલા અમુક દિવસોમાં છૂટાછવાયા વરસાદ અને પવનને કારણે ઘણા જિલ્લામાં મહત્તમ તાપમાનમાં નોંધપાત્ર ઘટાડો જોવા મળ્યો હતો.

હવામાન વિભાગે જાહેર કરેલી નવી આગાહી પ્રમાણે આગામી દિવસોમાં પણ કંઈક આવું જ વલણ જળવાઈ રહે તેવી શક્યતા છે, આ ઉપરાંત વિભાગે આગામી દિવસોમાં ચોમાસાની આગેકૂચ અંગે પણ શક્યતાઓ વ્યક્ત કરી છે.

ભારતમાં ચોમાસું ક્યાં પહોંચ્યું અને વેસ્ટર્ન ડિસ્ટર્બન્સની સ્થિતિ કેવી છે?

ઇમેજ સ્રોત,
IMD

હવામાન વિભાગે 3 જૂનના રોજ બપોરના 12 વાગ્યે જાહેર કરેલા બુલેટિન મુજબ દક્ષિણપશ્ચિમ ચોમાસું 4 જૂનની આસપાસ દક્ષિણપૂર્વ અને દક્ષિણ પશ્ચિમ અરબી સમુદ્ર, લક્ષદ્વીપ ટાપુ, કેરળ અને તામિલનાડુના વધુ ભાગો, દક્ષિણપશ્ચિમ, મધ્યપૂર્વ, મધ્યપશ્ચિમ અને ઉત્તરપૂર્વ બંગાળની ખાડી અને દક્ષિણપૂર્વ બંગાળની ખાડીની બાકીના વિસ્તારો તરફ આગળ વધે તે માટેની સાનુકૂળ સ્થિતિ છે.

2 જૂનના રોજ ઉત્તરપશ્ચિમ ઉત્તરપ્રદેશ અને આસપાસના વિસ્તારોમાં સાયક્લૉનિક સર્કુલેશન તરીકે જોવા મળેલું વેસ્ટર્ન ડિસ્ટર્બન્સ હવે પશ્ચિમ ઉત્તરપ્રદેશ અને તેની આસપાસના વિસ્તારો દરિયાની સપાટીથી અનુક્રમે 1.5 અને 5.8 કિમી ઉપર જળવાયેલું છે. જે હવે ગુજરાત માટે બિનમહત્ત્વપૂર્ણ બની ગયું છે.

ઉપરાંત ઉત્તર પાકિસ્તાન અને આસપાસના વિસ્તારોમાં દરિયાની સપાટીથી 3.1 કિમી ઉપર સાયક્લૉનિક સર્કુલેશન તરીકે નવું વેસ્ટર્ન ડિસ્ટર્બન્સ જોવા મળી રહ્યું છે. જેની સાથે દરિયાની સપાટીથી 5.8 કિમી ઉપર ટ્રફ પણ જળવાયેલું છે.

મધ્ય પાકિસ્તાન અને આસપાસના ઉત્તરપશ્ચિમ રાજસ્થાન પર દરિયાની સપાટીથી 0.9 કિમી ઉપર રહેલું અપર ઍર સાયક્લૉનિક સર્કુલેશન ઓછું માર્ક્ડ બન્યું છે.

Skip સૌથી વધારે વંચાયેલા સમાચાર and continue reading
સૌથી વધારે વંચાયેલા સમાચાર
'મારી પાસે 40 કિલો સોનું છે, મારા પતિ દર વર્ષે મારા માટે ત્રણ કિલો સોનું ખરીદે છે' - કોણ છે આ 'ગોલ્ડન ક્વીન'?
'રાત-દિવસ કોઈ જોયા કરે છે', સેક્સ વીડિયો માટે ઘરમાં ઘૂસીને લોકોની ફિલ્મ બનાવનાર પકડાયો, પીડિત મહિલા પર શું વીતી
વૈભવ સૂર્યવંશીની તોફાની બેટિંગ વિશે પાકિસ્તાનમાં લોકો શું બોલ્યા, પાડોશી દેશમાં અપાયું ખાસ નામ
'કોઈનું બાળક જોઉં તો થતું કે મારે પણ એક બાળક હોય', નસબંધી કરાવી ચૂકેલા નક્સલીઓની કહાણી

End of સૌથી વધારે વંચાયેલા સમાચાર

ઉત્તરપૂર્વ રાજસ્થાન અને આસપાસના વિસ્તાર આસપાસના વિસ્તારો પર દરિયાની સપાટીથી 1.5 કિમી ઉપર રહેલું અપર ઍર સાયક્લૉનિક સર્કુલેશન ઓછું માર્ક્ડ બન્યું છે.

ગુજરાતમાં નીચાણવાળા પ્રદેશોમાં દક્ષિણપશ્ચિમી અને પશ્ચિમી હવાઓનું જોર જળવાયેલું છે.

ગુજરાત : 'યુનિવર્સલ ઍફિડેવિટ' ફૉર્મેટ જાહેર, સરકારી સેવાઓ માટે જુદાં-જુદાં ઍફિડેવિટને બદલે હવે ફક્ત એક જ સોગંદનામું
ગુજરાતના કયા જિલ્લામાં આગામી દિવસોમાં વરસાદ પડી શકે?

ઇમેજ સ્રોત,
IMD

હવામાન વિભાગની આગાહી અનુસાર 4 જૂનના રોજ અરવલ્લી, પંચમહાલ, દાહોદ, મહીસાગર, વડોદરા, છોટા ઉદેપુર, નર્મદા, ભરૂચ, સુરત, ડાંગ, નવસારી, વલસાડ, તાપી, અમરેલી, ભાવનગર અને ગીર સોમનાથ ખાતે 40-50 કિમી પ્રતિ કલાકની ઝડપે પવન ફૂંકાવાની સાથોસાથ મધ્યમ ગાજવીજ સાથે હવળાથી મધ્યમ વરસાદ પડી શકે છે.

આ ઉપરાંત 5 જૂનના રોજ છોટા ઉદેપુર, નર્મદા, સુરત, ડાંગ, નવસારી, વલસાડ, તાપી, અમરેલી, ભાવનગર અને ગીર સોમનાથ ખાતે 40-50 કિમી પ્રતિ કલાકની ઝડપે વહેતા પવનો અને મધ્યમ ગાજવીજ સાથે હળવાથી મધ્યમ વરસાદ પડવાની શક્યતા વ્યક્ત કરાઈ છે.

6 જૂનના રોજ સુરત, ડાંગ, નવસારી, વલસાડ, તાપી, અમરેલી, ભાવનગર ખાતે ગીર સોમનાથ છૂટાછવાયાં સ્થળોએ હળવાથી મધ્ય વરસાદની શક્યતા છે.

એ જ દિવસે દક્ષિણ ગુજરાતના અન્ય જિલ્લા અને કચ્છ-સૌરાષ્ટ્રમાં શુષ્ક હવામાન જળવાઈ રહેવાની સંભાવના છે.

ટ્રમ્પને યુદ્ધ સમાપ્ત કરવું છે પણ હવે ઈરાન કેમ પીછેહઠ કરવા તૈયાર નથી, વાત ક્યાં આવીને અટકી છે?
2026માં ગુજરાત સહિત ભારતમાં ચોમાસું નબળું રહેશે?
બદલો Whatsapp
બીબીસી ન્યૂઝ ગુજરાતી હવે વૉટ્સઍપ પર

તમારા કામની સ્ટોરીઓ અને મહત્ત્વના સમાચારો હવે સીધા જ તમારા મોબાઇલમાં વૉટ્સઍપમાંથી વાંચો

વૉટ્સઍપ ચેનલ સાથે જોડાવ

Whatsapp કન્ટેન્ટ પૂર્ણ

વિશ્વ હવામાન સંસ્થા (વર્લ્ડ મિટિયૉરૉલૉજિકલ ઑર્ગેશનાઇઝેશ- ડબ્લ્યૂએમઓ) 2 જૂનના રોજ એક પ્રેસ રિલીઝ જાહેર કરી હતી. જે અનુસાર આગામી કેટલાક મહિના દરમિયાન મુખ્યત્વે ટ્રોપિકલ પૅસિફિકમાં સમુદ્રનું પાણી અસામાન્યપણે ગરમ થવાને કારણે અલ નીનોની સ્થિતિ વિકસી રહી છે, જે આગામી મહિનાઓમાં વિશ્વના ઘણા દેશોમાં તાપમાન અને વરસાદની પૅટર્નને અસર કરી શકે છે, જેના કારણે આગામી મહિનામાં તીવ્ર હવામાન સંબંધી અસરો જોવા મળી શકે છે.

ડબ્લ્યૂએમઓએ પોતાની પ્રેસ રિલીઝમાં સાઉથ એશિયન ક્લાઇમેટ આઉટલુક ફોરમને ટાંકીને લખ્યું હતું કે, "દક્ષિણ એશિયામાં પણ સામાન્યથી ઓછો વરસાદની આશંકા છે."

જે પ્રમાણે જૂન-સપ્ટેમ્બરના ચાર માસ દરમિયાન ભારત સહિત દક્ષિણ એશિયાના મોટા ભાગના દેશોમાં સામાન્ય કરતાં વધુ તાપમાન રહેવાની સંભાવના છે, આ સિવાય આ સમયગાળા દરમિયાન ભારતના મોટા ભાગનાં ક્ષેત્રોમાં સામાન્ય કરતાં ઓછો વરસાદ પડવાની સંભાવના વ્યક્ત કરાઈ છે.

નોંધનીય છે કે ગત 29 મેના રોજ ગુજરાત સહિત ભારતમાં હવામાન વિભાગે નબળા ચોમાસાની આગાહી કરી છે, જે મુજબ જૂનથી સપ્ટેમ્બર સુધી ચાર મહિનામાં સરેરાશ કરતાં ઓછો વરસાદ પડવાની સંભાવના છે.

બીબીસીના એક અહેવાલ અનુસાર પણ અલ નીનોની સ્થિતિ ભારતીય ચોમાસાને વધુ નબળું બનાવી શકે.

બીબીસી માટે કલેક્ટિવ ન્યૂઝરૂમનું પ્રકાશન

title: IMD issues Orange Alert for Thiruvananthapuram as Heavy Rains Lash District - News On AIR
article_text:
As pre-monsoon rains persist across Keralam, the India Meteorological Department has issued an Orange Alert for the Thiruvananthapuram district today as heavy rains lashed many parts of the district.

Waterlogging and traffic disruptions have been reported from several areas, affecting the movement of vehicles and pedestrians. The IMD has also issued a Yellow Alert for seven districts today and six districts tomorrow, forecasting isolated heavy rainfall. Fishermen have been advised not to venture into the sea along the Kerala and Lakshadweep coasts until June 3 due to adverse weather conditions. According to the IMD, atmospheric conditions are becoming increasingly favourable for the southwest monsoon to advance into parts of Kerala and Tamil Nadu within the next four to five days.

title: Southwest Monsoon Likely to Hit Kerala Within 24 Hours; Heavy Rainfall Forecast, Heatwave Eases in Telangana - heraldgoa.in
article_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast the onset of the southwest monsoon over Kerala within the next 24 hours, signaling the start of the country’s crucial monsoon season. The announcement, made on Wednesday afternoon, indicates that the monsoon is likely to reach Kerala earlier than its usual onset date of June 1.

Goa Travel Guide

According to the IMD, isolated areas across Kerala are expected to receive heavy to very heavy rainfall over the coming week as the monsoon strengthens over the region.

The arrival of the southwest monsoon over Kerala marks the beginning of the June-to-September rainy season, which plays a vital role in India’s agriculture and water resources.

Discover more Beaches & Islands SPORTS Headline News Feed

Meteorologists expect the monsoon system to gradually advance northward after entering Kerala, with Telangana likely to receive monsoon rains within about a week.

Meanwhile, the IMD has withdrawn heatwave alerts for Telangana, citing a noticeable drop in temperatures due to changing weather conditions associated with the advancing monsoon. The easing of heatwave conditions is expected to bring relief to residents who have been experiencing prolonged high temperatures in recent weeks.

(This story is published from a syndicated feed)

title: Heavy Rain Alert: अगले कुछ घंटों में मानसून की दस्तक, कई राज्यों में तेज आंधी के साथ भारी बारिश की चेतावनी - CNBC TV18
article_text:
होमफोटोएग्रीकल्चरHeavy Rain Alert: अगले कुछ घंटों में मानसून की दस्तक, कई राज्यों में तेज आंधी के साथ भारी बारिश की चेतावनी
Heavy Rain Alert: अगले कुछ घंटों में मानसून की दस्तक, कई राज्यों में तेज आंधी के साथ भारी बारिश की चेतावनी
SUMMARY

Heavy Rain Alert News: दक्षिण-पश्चिम मॉनसून अब केरल के दरवाजे पर पहुंच चुका है. भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) के अनुसार, मॉनसून आज शाम तक केरल में प्रवेश कर सकता है. मौसम विभाग ने इसको लेकर ऑरेंज अलर्ट जारी किया है. ऐसे में अगले कई दिनों तक देश के कई राज्यों में भारी से बहुत भारी बारिश होने की संभावना है.

By SHIVANI SHARMA  
June 4, 2026, 10:39:04 AM IST (Published)
1 Min Read
1 / 7

Heavy Rain Alert News: केरल में दक्षिण-पश्चिम मॉनसून के जल्द पहुंचने की संभावना जताई जा रही है. भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) ने कहा है कि मौसम की स्थिति मॉनसून के आने के लिए पूरी तरह अनुकूल बनी हुई है. अगले कुछ दिनों में राज्य के कई हिस्सों में तेज बारिश, गरज-चमक के साथ तूफानी मौसम और तेज हवाएं देखने को मिल सकती हैं. (Image Source: AI-Generated)

2 / 7

मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, केरल और लक्षद्वीप क्षेत्र में अगले 5 से 7 दिनों तक भारी से बहुत भारी बारिश हो सकती है. इस दौरान कुछ जगहों पर बिजली गिरने और आंधी-तूफान जैसी स्थिति बनने की भी आशंका है. कई जिलों में हालात को देखते हुए ऑरेंज और येलो अलर्ट जारी किया गया है. (Image Source: AI-Generated)

title: Monsoon Alert: केरल पहुंचा मानसून, अगले 7 दिन तक भारी बारिश का अलर्ट, मिलेगी गर्मी से राहत - Daily Samvad
article_text:
डेली संवाद, चंडीगढ़। Monsoon Alert: भीषण गर्मी के बीच एक राहत भरी खबर है। केरल में दक्षिण-पश्चिम मानसून आ चुका है। भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) की इस घोषणा के बाद केरल के कई हिस्सों में भारी बारिश शुरू हो गई है।
मौसम विभाग ने भविष्यवाणी की है कि आने वाले दिनों में मानसून तेजी से आगे बढ़ेगा और देश के विशाल क्षेत्र में फैल जाएगा। भारतीय मौसम विभाग (IMD) ने पुष्टि की कि दक्षिण-पश्चिम मानसून आधिकारिक तौर पर केरल में प्रवेश कर चुका है।
इसके साथ ही लक्षद्वीप और तटीय क्षेत्रों में भी बारिश तेज हो गई है। मौसम विभाग ने 4 से 9 जून के बीच केरल में भारी से बहुत भारी बारिश की चेतावनी जारी की है। कई स्थानों पर 7 से 20 सेंटीमीटर तक बारिश होने की संभावना है।
मौसम विभाग (IMD) के अनुसार, तिरुवनंतपुरम, कोल्लम, पथानामथिट्टा, अलाप्पुझा, कोट्टायम, इडुक्की, एर्नाकुलम और त्रिशूर जिलों के लिए ‘ऑरेंज अलर्ट’ जारी किया गया है। विभाग ने चेतावनी दी है कि भारी बारिश के कारण इन क्षेत्रों में जलभराव का खतरा है और इससे जनजीवन बाधित हो सकता है।
मानसून शुरू होने के बाद से केरल का मौसम पूरी तरह बदल गया है। कई जिलों में लगातार बारिश हो रही है और तापमान में काफी गिरावट दर्ज की गई है। मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, आने वाले दिनों में उत्तरी केरल के कोझिकोड, वायनाड, कन्नूर और कासरगोड जिलों में भारी बारिश होने की संभावना है।
